hero.title: JOSOOR
hero.subtitle: Transformation Intelligence
hero.sectors: Operating System for the Public Sector
hero.badge:
Delivery at the Speed of Ambition
Vision 2030
hero.pitch: Two contradictions that public sector entities cannot ignore while adhering to regulatory requirements of accountability and oversight:
pitchPoints: Delaying scaled adoption of AI within national directives
Risks of using AI based on probabilistic models
bestPracticesPoints: Provide an enterprise architecture of deterministic software that governs AI tasks
Assign it specific tasks (e.g. complex data processing) with the tools needed to achieve them
hero.intelligenceDesc: Rapid analysis of complex relations, enriched with context
hero.trustDesc: Consistent outputs you can depend on and trace in full
hero.middleTagline: Building the bridges from Strategy to Execution, one bridge at a time.

aitoia.title: Over 50% of Enterprises fail in AI adoption
aitoia.subtitle: Don't start your AI project before establishing the success factors
aitoia.challengeSubtitle: How can AI adoption in Enterprises scale with such unfavorable odds?
reasons: Data is not ready - Years of silos, both structured and unstructured
Lack of business value - Chasing the hype vs solving business problems
Escalating total cost of ownership - Underestimating running investments
Poor change management - AI is still viewed as a replacement tool
Responsible AI is an afterthought - causing incidents that erode credibility
aitoia.consequence: Ignoring AI means falling behind. Adopting it without structure means unmanaged risk. The question isn't whether to act — but how.
aitoia.innovationTitle: Writing the Book for Public Sector AI
aitoia.innovationSubtitle: We architect solutions tailored to your reality. Your first step is to contact us below to define the most suited plan for your situation.

iaItems.desc: Context is everything. Knowing your operating model reality from data, systems, to adoption paints a picture.
Different entities are at different stages of their journeys. We align with these dynamic priorities.
Between what's possible and what's needed is a set of high-impact use cases.

claims.title: See the difference
claims.subtitle: What makes JOSOOR fundamentally different
claims.canvasLabel2: From Black Box to Glass Box
claims.canvasDesc: Every structure is machine-native. Every interface is human-intuitive.
imageDescs: Agents draw from a curated Vision 2030 knowledge base spanning all sectors through a dedicated MCP Server. Every query combines national priorities with your entity's own data — decisions are never made in a vacuum. The result: recommendations that are aligned by design, not by afterthought.

The first real solution to Bridging Strategy to Execution. Sectors, capabilities, KPIs, risks, and projects — all connected in one living model. Risk governance is built into the structure, not bolted on. This is the IP that makes everything else work.

Three Master Agents in a continuous loop:|Observe: Are we on track? Strategic PMO.|Decide: Risks caught early, prioritized in one click. Risk Specialist.|Execute: Actionable plan before the meeting ends. Planning Specialist.

Human-First Architecture with AI-in-the-Loop for deterministic decision-making. Agents are powered by a 4-layer memory, a scalable Vision database, specialized MCP tools, and a source-of-truth World Model. Every output is traceable. Every recommendation is explainable.
Homegrown in KSA. Model-agnostic. No vendor lock-in. Full sovereignty from day one.|Your data never leaves your infrastructure. Your AI answers to your rules, not someone else's terms of service.

strategyHouse.title: Trusted Frameworks on Intelligent Foundations
strategyHouse.subtitle: Transforming Complexity into your most valuable asset
elementDescs: Transforming a sector means changing how it works — who invests, who operates, and what they focus on. Governments steer this through policy and regulation, incentives, and awareness. JOSOOR maps these tools to the players, investments, and activities they influence — so you see which levers are moving the sector toward national objectives and which ones are not.

You cannot transform a sector without transforming the entity that manages it. The capabilities needed to design policies, enforce regulations, and run incentive programs must be built in sync with the sector's evolution. JOSOOR tracks both in parallel — so your operating model is always ready for the mandate it serves.

Too many initiatives, limited resources, and high stakes. Portfolio management is how you maximize impact from every investment while containing risk. JOSOOR scores ROI across projects, assets, capabilities, and leadership time — so you know where to double down and where to stop spending.

Agents draw from a curated Vision 2030 knowledge base spanning all sectors through a dedicated MCP Server. Every query combines national priorities with your entity's own data — decisions are never made in a vacuum. The result: recommendations that are aligned by design, not by afterthought.

The first real solution to Bridging Strategy to Execution. Sectors, capabilities, KPIs, risks, and projects — all connected in one living model. Risk governance is built into the structure, not bolted on. This is the IP that makes everything else work.

Three Master Agents in a continuous loop:|Observe: Are we on track? Strategic PMO.|Decide: Risks caught early, prioritized in one click. Risk Specialist.|Execute: Actionable plan before the meeting ends. Planning Specialist.

Human-First Architecture with AI-in-the-Loop for deterministic decision-making. Agents are powered by a 4-layer memory, a scalable Vision database, specialized MCP tools, and a source-of-truth World Model. Every output is traceable. Every recommendation is explainable.
Homegrown in KSA. Model-agnostic. No vendor lock-in. Full sovereignty from day one.|Your data never leaves your infrastructure. Your AI answers to your rules, not someone else's terms of service.

architecture.title: Designed around data you have today
architecture.intro: No new data lake. No 18-month integration. JOSOOR is an overlay that builds bridges between your existing systems and stakeholders — a true information superhighway traversed by powerful LLMs, transforming data complexity into strategic insights and siloed stakeholders into an aligned powerhouse.

engines.title: BUILD — Are we building on time?
engines.desc: For each capability under construction, checks every project closing its gaps. Calculates build exposure from real dates. No AI opinion — math.

engines.title: OPERATE — Are we running healthy?
engines.desc: Scores people + process + tools. Two consecutive drops, even while green? Forced amber. Catches decline before red.

founderLetter.title: Founder's Letter: Origins
paragraphs: Just over two years ago was my first introduction to GenAI. What started as a basic ask to ChatGPT to design a training program to teach me "AI", continued non-stop over the entire weekend to end with contemplating how unlocking the human potential is not in the pursuit of answers but rather the articulation of the right questions. And from that came the concept of the Living Transformation Network, a semi-sentient network of AIs carrying one mission to improve humanity and life by taking over the complexity of society and daring us to think.
"What do we do with all the spare time we will have?"
That experience changed me forever. I even published the wild interaction in a LinkedIn article, with the aim of helping other skeptics or on the fence seniors to make the jump and embrace AI.

Since then, I have been pursuing that specific concept. Which at its core is "how to make transformation management push back and control the complexity beast?" I continued to design the model itself. Yet after six months, even with a sound model that took all the complexity and jammed it into a relational database, the AI tech was still not there yet, and a solution then meant investing the old way (licenses, infra., coders etc.).
So I kept it on a slow burner, refining and tweaking based on real life client setups I encounter.

Until a few months back. GenAI started to make significant leaps in capabilities, and the AI development tools and communities advanced with more "no coding" solutions. Suddenly, the fire power needed to navigate the complex maze of relations was not only available, but remarkably affordable. Not only that, but cloud hosting had also a breakthrough with Azure certified by the government after years of a no-cloud hosting policy.
I had no choice, no excuse and no regrets in chasing this dream.

All my career I was a pioneer, but for others' benefit. This was my chance to flip the script and pioneer with no constraints. In fact, part of the fuel behind this was all the bottled up "red-tape" frustration. I am pursuing this all the way Inshallah.
I hope once the concept and its national benefits clicks, you will join me in this journey, whether as an Architect or a Builder, so we give it the best chance of success.

controls.title: Control Signals
controls.subtitle: System integration — is intent flowing end-to-end?
controls.steeringSub: Where governance pressure concentrates from Policy/Performance into Capabilities
controls.riskSub: Which PolicyTools or KPIs are threatened by {{mode}} risk
controls.deliverySub: Build demand feasibility: where load accumulates across Org/Process/IT → Projects → Adoption
controls.integritySub: Where intent or control "dies" by measuring leakage at each node
controls.flagRiskConcentration: PT3: Pricing Framework - RISK CONCENTRATION (3+ risks >50%)
controls.flagRiskBroken: PT1: Desalination Policy - BROKEN GOVERNANCE (exposure 70%, inactive link)
controls.flagDeliverySaturation: Cap1: Plant Operations - SATURATION (5+ active projects)
controls.flagDeliveryBottleneck: Adopt3: QC Change - ADOPTION BOTTLENECK (3+ projects, <1 adoption)
controls.flagIntegrityBroken: Obj3: Service Quality - BROKEN CHAIN (30% leakage)
controls.flagIntegritySink: Cap2: Distribution - SINK CAPABILITY (35% leakage, high inflow, low outflow)

reporting.title: Reporting Desk
reporting.subtitle: Control outcome tracking & standard report generation
reporting.interventionResult: Intervention Successful — Risk Reduced from Red to Amber
reporting.delayRiskAcceptable: Schedule delay risk — acceptable within tolerance
reporting.processGapMonitoring: Process gap under monitoring — next review Q2
reporting.distributionExecutiveReview: Distribution capability requires executive review — persistent load imbalance
reporting.executiveSummaryText: Water sector capabilities show improving trajectory with 3 of 5 critical capabilities stabilized in Q1. Plant operations intervention yielded measurable risk reduction. Distribution remains under structural stress requiring executive attention.
reporting.performanceTrendText: Steady improvement across operational capabilities since intervention. Risk exposure declining at 4% per quarter average.
reporting.areasOfConcernText: Distribution capability shows persistent load imbalance affecting downstream KPIs. 2 policy tools require re-evaluation.
reporting.recommendationsText: Escalate distribution review to steering committee. Initiate policy tool refresh for PT-03 and PT-07. Continue monitoring plant ops stabilization.
reporting.confidenceDescription: Based on data freshness, completeness, and cross-source validation.
reporting.missingAdoptionData: Policy tool adoption data > 30 days old

insightPanel.baselineHealth: Baseline health status across all tracked capabilities in the current portfolio view.
insightPanel.selectOverlay: Select an overlay to analyze specific capability dimensions.
insightPanel.riskExposureDesc: Identifies capabilities facing delivery delays or operational health degradation based on current risk indicators.
insightPanel.riskExposureExplanation: RED = ≥15% exposure. AMBER = 5-15% exposure. GREEN = <5% exposure.
insightPanel.externalPressureDesc: Highlights capabilities under heavy regulatory or performance mandate load from external stakeholders.
insightPanel.footprintStressDesc: Reveals capabilities with significant imbalances across organizational structure, processes, and technology assets.
insightPanel.changeSaturationDesc: Detects capabilities experiencing excessive change velocity that may compromise adoption and integration quality.
insightPanel.changeSaturationExplanation: Capabilities overwhelmed with too many concurrent projects.
insightPanel.trendEarlyWarningDesc: Surfaces capabilities showing sustained health decline patterns over recent measurement cycles for proactive intervention.

detailPanel.regressionDimensionsDesc: Health of the 3 operational dimensions. Weakest dimension drives regression risk.

detailPanel.stageGateDesc: All 3 dimensions must reach the target stage for activation.

ont_executive_prompt: Based on this data, produce a comprehensive executive report on sector health: where are the biggest exposures? What are the top 3 urgent priorities this quarter? And what is the minister-level summary?
ont_column_prompt: Based on this data, analyze "{{colName}}": what is its current standing? What are the key issues? What dependencies affect it? And what are the actionable recommendations?
ont_micro_category: Category: {{label}} | Status: {{status}} | Total: {{count}} | Critical: {{redCount}} | At Risk: {{amberCount}}
ont_micro_item_detail: Item: {{name}} | ID: {{id}} | Status: {{rag}} | Depends on: {{upLinks}} | Affects: {{downLinks}}
ont_impact_cascade: "{{name}}" impacts {{impact}} downstream KPIs — unresolved, this will cascade across sector outputs
ont_no_data_for_node: No data available for this node
ont_from_no_dest: {{count}} {{fromLbl}} with no destination + {{bastards}} {{toLbl}} with no source
ont_micro_landscape_prompt: In 3-4 short bullets: Why is {{label}} in this state? What is the single biggest risk right now? What should be done first?
ont_micro_block1_prompt: In 3-4 short bullets: What connects these top issues? Which one, if fixed, would improve the others? What is the recommended sequence of action?
ont_micro_item_prompt: In 3-4 short bullets: Why is "{{name}}" flagged? What upstream dependencies are causing this? What is the downstream impact if left unresolved? What is the one action to take now?
